text: We propose to study prompt injection attacks in skill-based LLM agents. Modern agent architectures increasingly organize functionality as reusable skills—high-level workflows that orchestrate multiple tools. This design introduces a new attack surface: an attacker can compromise the file of skill (i.e., skill.md) to manipulate the agent to select unintended skills or inject malicious instructions into skill workflows, leading to privilege escalation or sensitive data exfiltration.
